regime.note: Anti-cheat/MP regime: config is NOT the decisive lever; success is gated by whether the anti-cheat vendor enabled a Proton runtime (exogenous). High recent yes-rate => the runtime IS currently accepted on Linux. Treat config params as comfort/perf tweaks, and lean on the recency-windowed yes-rate as the go/no-go.

scoring_notes.catalog_relevance: max(0, log(smoothed_lift)) * recency_weighted_prevalence  -- game-SPECIFICITY ordering only; NOT a benefit/risk score.
scoring_notes.smoothed_lift: (support + k) / (denom*base_rate + k), k=1.0  -- observed vs expected, shrunk toward 1 at low support.
scoring_notes.selection_bias_warning: Users add launch options when STRUGGLING, so yes_rate_with_param and verdict_delta are weak/negatively-biased signals of efficacy (see -dx11). Lean on enrichment (lift) + recency for the catalog; defer benefit/risk to the annotation KB. All sub-scores are stored raw so a consumer can re-weight.
scoring_notes.basis: launchopts params scored vs reports-with-launchOptions; proton variant/custom/notes params scored vs their own present-population (see background_denominators).
